channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.description: Most engineering teams know they should care about reliability. But when it comes to defining what "reliable" actually means, things get fuzzy fast. According to a 2023 report by Xurrent, 74% of...

item.title: Building Safe LangChain Agents with Scope Verification
item.description: LangChain is great at building agents that act. Search the web. Write files. Send emails. Call APIs. The framework handles orchestration beautifully. What it doesn't handle: authorization. Your...

item.title: Why your Supabase mutations lie about their errors

item.title: SLO examples for financial services: what good performance looks like in fintech
item.description: Every financial services company knows what a failed transaction costs. The number is immediate, calculable, and visible in the next day's report. What's less visible — but equally costly — is the...

item.title: System Design 101: Consistent Hashing Explained
item.description: Consistent Hashing What Is It? Consistent Hashing is a distributed hashing scheme that operates independently of the number of servers in a cluster. It ensures that when servers are added or...

item.title: The New IT Career Formula: AI + English + Human Skills
item.description: AI tools are changing software development fast. Developers are producing more code, managers are coding again, and companies want smaller teams to deliver more results. But technical skills alone...

item.title: Beyond Static Code: Building an AI-Powered "VC Critic" on Somnia
item.description: For years, smart contracts have been described as "smart," but in reality, they have been static. They could move assets and follow rigid if/then rules, but they were "blind" to human language,...

item.title: There’s an internet choke point in the Middle East — is the solution in the North Pole?
item.description: The vast majority of the world's data - emails, financial transactions, the internet - is carried by fiber optic cables that run along the ocean floor and converge at a few narrow choke points....

item.title: Amazon’s ultrafast 30-minute deliveries are now available in more cities
item.description: Following a limited test run in parts of Seattle and Philadelphia that started last December, Amazon Now is officially available to millions of Amazon shoppers across the United States. In...

item.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis for driving on flooded roads
item.description: Waymo is recalling its autonomous driving software that allowed its vehicles to drive on flooded roads. The recall affects 3,791 vehicles that operate using Waymo's fifth and sixth generation...

item.title: Canvas owner reaches ‘agreement’ with hackers to secure stolen data
item.description: Instructure, the company behind the Canvas learning management platform, says it has "reached an agreement" with hackers that breached its systems last week to prevent stolen data from being...

item.title: Dessn raises $6M for its production focused design tool
item.description: A new startup called Dessn has raised $6M to build AI-powered design tools that work directly with production codebases.

item.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric...

item.title: Spotify’s latest ‘Wrapped’ covers your entire music history
item.description: Spotify is celebrating its 20th anniversary by giving listeners a "fully personalized look at their entire music history." Starting today, Spotify mobile app users can access a new Wrapped-like...

item.title: The Download: a Nobel winner on AI, and the case for fixing everything
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning...

item.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
item.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are...

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent supply chain attack spree, has been linked to the compromise of the npm and PyPI packages from TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails...

item.title: AI voice startup Vapi hits $500M valuation after winning Amazon Ring over 40 rivals
item.description: Vapi says its enterprise business has grown 10-fold since early 2025 as companies shift customer support and sales calls to AI agents.

item.title: Copy.Fail Linux Vulnerability
item.description: This is the worst Linux vulnerability in years. TL;DR copy.fail is a Linux kernel local privilege escalation, not a browser or clipboard attack. Disclosed by Theori on 29 April 2026 with a working...

item.title: How to watch Google’s Android Show: I/O Edition today
item.description: Google I/O is still a week away, but Google's big announcements kick off today with the "Android Show: I/O Edition," where it's expected to announce the major Android ecosystem highlights coming...

item.title: eBay rejects GameStop’s $56 billion acquisition bid
item.description: eBay has officially rejected GameStop's offer to buy the online marketplace for $56 billion, citing concerns around "operational risks" and how the acquisition will be financed. In a letter to...

item.title: Why Agentic AI Is Security's Next Blind Spot
item.description: Agentic AI is already running in production environments across many organizations today. It is executing tasks, consuming data, and taking actions — most likely without meaningful involvement...

item.title: These new Roombas are smaller and cheaper
item.description: iRobot has announced a new line of Roombas, a year after it debuted its first lidar-based robot vacuums. This week, it introduced eight robot vacuums that improve on its current lineup and feature...

item.title: OpenAI Launches Daybreak for AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation
item.description: OpenAI has launched Daybreak, a new cybersecurity initiative that brings together frontier artificial intelligence (AI) model capabilities and Codex Security to help organizations identify and...

item.title: Sony upgraded its wearable AC so it’s cooler and better at hugging your neck
item.description: Sony has announced a new version of the wearable personal air conditioner the company first revealed in 2019 ahead of the 2020 Tokyo Olympics. The latest version is an upgrade to last year's Pro...

item.title: iOS 26.5 Brings Default End-to-End Encrypted RCS Messaging Between iPhone and Android
item.description: Apple on Monday officially released iOS 26.5 with support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) to Rich Communication Services (RCS) in beta as part of a "cross-industry effort" to replace traditional...
